name: Tantivy
description: Tantivy is a full-text search engine library written in Rust, inspired by Apache Lucene. It provides inverted indexing, BM25 scoring, tokenization, fuzzy search, and faceted aggregation, all within a single embeddable library with zero external dependencies. Indexes persist to disk using a Lucene-like segment-based architecture with background merging, making it suitable for applications that need fast, relevance-ranked full-text search over local document collections.
format_on_disk: Inverted Index Segments (multi-file, Lucene-like segment architecture)
core_strengths: Lucene-equivalent full-text search with BM25 relevance scoring
Zero external dependencies — pure Rust, compiles to a single library
Segment-based architecture with lock-free concurrent reads
Pluggable tokenizers (built-in: simple, raw, whitespace, N-gram, regex)
Fuzzy search, phrase queries, range queries, and boolean combinations
best_use_cases: Full-text search over local document collections
Building a search engine for a static site or documentation generator
In-app search for CLI tools and desktop applications
Embedding search into a Rust-based data pipeline or ETL tool
Local knowledge base and personal wiki search
remarks: Tantivy does not provide a query language parser for user-facing search queries by default — the query parser's error handling is not designed for end-user consumption out of the box. Indexing is single-writer; only one IndexWriter can exist per index at a time. Python bindings (tantivy-py) are available but are a separate community-maintained project.
